name: Buriat

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Buriat (buri1258).

notes: Skeleton. Buriat (Buryat) is a Mongolic language written in a Cyrillic alphabet that extends Russian with three letters: Ү (near-close back rounded), Ө (close-mid front rounded) and Һ (h). The map covers the base Cyrillic inventory plus these and encodes phonemic long vowels, which Buriat writes as doubled vowel letters (аа, өө, үү …), via longest-match digraph entries. Vowel harmony and the reduced quality of unstressed vowels are conditioned rather than spelled and are left unencoded; Russian loanword letters (в ф ц щ) keep their Russian values.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Buriat (buri1258)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
